mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is stable and the policy move is not a response to an acute growth collapse.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is declining on a 3-month basis, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above its recent trend marks late-cycle labor deterioration and weak expected earnings, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when unemployment rises after a low-level plateau rather than during recession already priced by markets.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect flips positive for two consecutive years in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales growth indicates stronger near-term household demand and revenue momentum, which should support equity prices over the following week. The effect is immediate because markets reprice sales-sensitive earnings estimates faster than macro output data.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of the 5-day forward return is negative in more than half of months where RSAFS accelerates on a 3-month basis, or if IC is indistinguishable from zero over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production rebound above trend signals improving factory output and inventory restocking, which raises cyclically sensitive earnings expectations and supports S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when production gains are driven by transitory catch-up after a prior shock and do not broaden into demand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 1.0% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods after the trigger, or if the signal loses all predictive power when tested outside recovery months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in housing starts and retail sales identifies a broad-based demand upswing in rate-sensitive and consumer sectors, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The interaction matters because each series alone can reflect sector-specific noise, but together they indicate economy-wide demand acceleration.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal does not outperform either single-series proxy in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if triggered joint-strength periods fail to produce positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is rising while the policy rate remains pinned near zero, real rates compress and liquidity is abundant, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect depends on the gap between inflation and policy, not on inflation alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1.5 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal fails to improve forecast accuracy over CPIAUCSL alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not above baseline in the triggered low-real-rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with falling unemployment indicates strengthening labor demand and income growth, which should lift equity returns over the next month through better consumer spending and earnings expectations. The signal breaks down when payroll gains are offset by rising unemployment, implying noisy labor churn rather than true improvement.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not deliver better than 60% hit rate on positive 20-day returns, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth combined with rising unemployment signals demand recession and earnings deterioration, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This regime-specific effect is strongest when output is shrinking and labor slack is increasing together, confirming a true macro slowdown rather than a temporary data wobble.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint downturn signal does not predict negative 60-day returns better than either proxy alone, or if positive forward returns dominate during periods flagged by shrinking GDPC1 and rising UNRATE.
